description: California defines hazardous waste per 22 CCR §66261.3. A waste is hazardous if it is RCRA-listed, RCRA-characteristic, OR meets California-only criteria.
California Toxicity Criteria (non-RCRA): Soluble Threshold Limit Concentration (STLC) and Total Threshold Limit Concentration (TTLC) per 22 CCR §66261.24.

questions.text: Has Waste Extraction Test (WET) / STLC testing been performed?
Provide STLC results for all applicable constituents

description: California requires use of the Uniform Hazardous Waste Manifest AND registration with the DTSC manifest system. Non-RCRA hazardous waste ALSO requires a manifest in California.

questions.text: Is the facility registered with the DTSC manifest tracking system?

description: California-registered hazardous waste haulers required. DTSC hauler registration number must be on manifest.

questions.text: Provide the transporter's California DTSC Registered Hazardous Waste Hauler number

description: Washington State classifies wastes as 'dangerous waste' under WAC 173-303. This is broader than federal RCRA — includes state-only DW categories (WT02, WP02, etc.).

questions.text: Has a Washington State dangerous waste determination been performed per WAC 173-303-070?

description: Washington uses its own toxicity criteria (book designation, WAC 173-303-080 through -100) in addition to federal characteristic testing.

questions.text: Has book designation per WAC 173-303-080 been evaluated?
Provide Washington toxicity criteria results if applicable

description: Washington requires manifests for ALL dangerous waste (state-only + RCRA). State waste codes must appear on manifest.
Texas adopts federal RCRA with TCEQ-specific modifications per 30 TAC Chapter 335.
Texas requires Class 1 industrial solid waste determination for non-hazardous industrial wastes that may still require special management.

questions.text: Has a Class 1 industrial solid waste determination been performed?

description: TCEQ requires annual waste summary reports from LQGs and SQGs.

questions.text: Has the annual TCEQ waste summary report been filed?

description: New York adopts RCRA and adds 6 NYCRR Part 371 state-specific listed wastes and characteristic thresholds.
NYSDEC requires waste characterization data form (DEC Form) to accompany profile.

questions.text: Has the NYSDEC waste characterization data form been completed?

description: New York requires hazardous waste manifests for both RCRA and state-listed wastes. Additional Part 372 manifest copies required.

questions.text: Confirm that additional Part 372 manifest copies will be provided to NYSDEC

description: Illinois adopts federal RCRA per 35 IAC Part 721 with Illinois-specific special waste categories.
Illinois EPA requires special waste classification for non-hazardous industrial wastes. Special waste stream authorization required.

questions.text: Has an Illinois special waste stream authorization been obtained?

description: Illinois requires special waste hauling permits for non-hazardous special waste transport.

questions.text: Does the transporter hold an Illinois special waste hauling permit?

description: New Jersey DEP imposes additional hazardous waste listings per N.J.A.C. 7:26G beyond federal RCRA.
NJ DEP requires the NJ Special Waste Manifest for non-hazardous industrial waste and additional copies for hazardous waste.

questions.text: Will the NJ DEP Special Waste Manifest be used for this shipment?

description: NJ DEP requires source reduction planning for hazardous waste generators.

questions.text: Has the facility submitted a source reduction plan to NJ DEP?

description: Puerto Rico adopts federal RCRA with modifications. Generators must register with DNER in addition to EPA.

questions.text: Has this facility registered with DNER as a hazardous waste generator?

description: Puerto Rico requires Spanish-language manifest copies for local transport.

questions.text: Will this shipment remain entirely within Puerto Rico?
